findings.description: Exceptional CI/CD infrastructure with 24 GitHub Actions workflows covering linting, testing, releases, and automation
findings.evidence: Code search found 24 workflow files including: shared_lint.yml, runtime_prereleases.yml, compiler_prereleases_manual.yml, shared_stale.yml, shared_check_maintainer.yml, shared_cleanup_merged_branch_caches.yml, shared_label_core_team_prs.yml, and Discord notification workflows. Recent commits from 2026-04-17 to 2026-04-22 show active merging with passing checks.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices. Consider adding a .github/workflows/README.md to document workflow purposes for new contributors.

findings.description: Highly active repository with recent, well-documented commits from diverse maintainers
findings.evidence: list_commits returned 10 commits from 2026-04-17 to 2026-04-22. Recent merges include: 'Fix FragmentInstance listener leak' (#36047) by @Dhakshin2007, 'Suggest correct casing for credentialless iframe attribute' (#36322) by @vmx906, 'Add credentialless as recognized boolean attribute' (#36148) by @vmx906, 'Double invoke Effects in StrictMode after Fast Refresh' (#35962) by @eps1lon. All commits have detailed messages and PR references.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current commit message quality and merge frequency.

findings.description: Comprehensive quality tooling with ESLint, Prettier, Flow, TypeScript, and automated PR checks
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows: .eslintrc.js (20,976 bytes), .prettierrc.js, .prettierignore, babel.config.js, babel.config-ts.js, dangerfile.js (8,470 bytes for PR automation). package.json includes: eslint@^7.7.0, prettier@^3.3.3, flow-bin@^0.279.0, typescript@^5.4.3, danger@^11.2.3, @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin, eslint-plugin-react-internal (custom rules).
findings.recommendation: Excellent setup. No changes needed.

findings.description: Excellent PR management with active triage, CLA enforcement, and no stale PR backlog
findings.evidence: list_pull_requests returned 10 open PRs, all created within 5 days (2026-04-17 to 2026-04-22). All PRs have 'CLA Signed' label. Examples: PR #36328 (draft, @eps1lon, React Core Team), #36324 (@thomasbuilds, eslint-plugin fix), #36321 (@starboyvarun, hydration mismatch fix). All PRs include detailed descriptions, test plans, and root cause analyses. Updated timestamps show recent activity (e.g., #36321 updated 2026-04-22, #36314 updated 2026-04-20).
findings.recommendation: Continue current PR review practices and automated labeling.

findings.description: Well-organized monorepo structure with clear separation of concerns and comprehensive documentation
findings.evidence: get_repository_tree returned 36 root items including: packages/ (monorepo workspace), compiler/ (React Compiler subproject), scripts/ (build/release automation), fixtures/ (test fixtures), .github/ (workflows, templates, dependabot.yml). Documentation files: README.md (5,317 bytes), CHANGELOG.md (265,863 bytes), CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, LICENSE, MAINTAINERS. package.json shows workspaces: ['packages/*'].
findings.recommendation: Maintain current structure. Consider adding architectural decision records (ADRs) for major design choices.

findings.description: Enterprise-grade dependency management with lock file, version pinning, and automated updates
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows yarn.lock (823,046 bytes). package.json includes: packageManager: 'yarn@1.22.22', 100+ devDependencies, resolutions for 'react-is' and 'jsdom@22.1.0'. .github/dependabot.yml present. Scripts: 'flow-typed-install', 'check-release-dependencies'. .nvmrc file present (Node version pinning).
findings.recommendation: Continue using Dependabot for automated dependency updates. Current lock file practices are excellent.

findings.description: Sophisticated test infrastructure with multiple test channels and comprehensive coverage
findings.evidence: package.json scripts include: 'test', 'test-stable', 'test-www', 'test-classic', 'test-build-devtools', 'test-dom-fixture'. jest.config with testRegex: '/scripts/jest/dont-run-jest-directly\.js$'. Dependencies: jest@^29.4.2, jest-cli@^29.4.2, jest-environment-jsdom@^29.4.2, jest-diff@^29.4.2. Root tree shows fixtures/ directory for test data.
findings.recommendation: Excellent test coverage. Maintain multi-channel testing approach.

findings.description: Advanced build system with multiple release channels and automated prerelease workflows
findings.evidence: package.json scripts: 'build', 'build-for-devtools', 'build-for-flight-dev', 'build-for-flight-prod', 'lint-build', 'generate-inline-fizz-runtime', 'download-build', 'publish-prereleases'. Dependencies: rollup@^3.29.5, @rollup/plugin-babel, @rollup/plugin-commonjs, google-closure-compiler@^20230206.0.0, gzip-size@^5.1.1. Workflows: runtime_prereleases.yml, compiler_prereleases_manual.yml, runtime_prereleases_manual.yml.
findings.recommendation: Current build automation is industry-leading. Continue leveraging multiple release channels for staged rollouts.

findings.description: Excellent community documentation and contributor onboarding
findings.evidence: get_file_contents(.github) returned: PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (1,715 bytes), ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ directory, dependabot.yml (201 bytes). Root tree shows: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (3,541 bytes), CONTRIBUTING.md (208 bytes), SECURITY.md (400 bytes), MAINTAINERS (219 bytes), .git-blame-ignore-revs (82 bytes for blame hygiene).
findings.recommendation: Exemplary community practices. No improvements needed.

findings.description: CONTRIBUTING.md is surprisingly small (208 bytes) for a project of this scale
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows CONTRIBUTING.md at 208 bytes, which is minimal compared to other documentation files (README.md: 5,317 bytes, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md: 3,541 bytes, CHANGELOG.md: 265,863 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Consider expanding CONTRIBUTING.md with detailed contributor guidelines, development setup instructions, and coding standards. The current file may redirect to external documentation, but inline guidance would improve contributor experience.

findings.description: The repository has 823 open issues, which is substantial but proportional to the project scale. Recent issues are consistently labeled with type and status tags within 24-48 hours of creation.
findings.evidence: list_issues API returned totalCount: 823 open issues. Recent samples: #36327 (2026-04-22, labeled 'Type: Bug', 'Status: Unconfirmed'), #36320 (2026-04-20, 'Status: Unconfirmed'), #36315 (2026-04-18, 'Type: Bug', 'Component: Developer Tools'). All recent issues show structured labeling.
findings.recommendation: Continue current triage practices. Consider implementing automated stale issue detection after 90 days to reduce backlog, though current 'Resolution: Stale' usage indicates this is already partially addressed.

findings.description: The repository demonstrates excellent contributor diversity with at least 7-10 distinct active committers in recent history, significantly reducing bus factor risk.
findings.evidence: list_commits (last 10 commits, 2026-04-17 to 2026-04-22) shows authors: Dhakshin2007, vmx906, jackpope, zeyap, mofeiZ, eps1lon (appearing 3 times), and web-flow. Commit messages reference PRs from diverse contributors. CHANGELOG.md credits hundreds of contributors including @acdlite, @gaearon, @sebmarkbage, @sophiebits, @bvaughn, and many external contributors.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current contributor onboarding practices. Document succession planning in MAINTAINERS file if not already present to ensure long-term sustainability.

findings.description: Complete governance documentation is present, including CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (Contributor Covenant 1.4), CONTRIBUTING.md, MAINTAINERS, SECURITY.md, and an exceptionally detailed CHANGELOG.md covering all releases from 2013 to 2025.
findings.evidence: get_repository_tree confirmed files: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (3541 bytes), CONTRIBUTING.md (208 bytes), MAINTAINERS (219 bytes), SECURITY.md (400 bytes), CHANGELOG.md (265,863 bytes). get_file_contents for CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md shows adoption of Contributor Covenant 1.4 with enforcement contact 'opensource-conduct@fb.com'. CONTRIBUTING.md links to external guide at reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html.
findings.recommendation: Excellent governance foundation. Consider inlining key contributing steps in CONTRIBUTING.md for offline reference, though external linking is acceptable for large projects. Optionally add GOVERNANCE.md to clarify decision-making processes.

findings.description: CONTRIBUTING.md is minimal (208 bytes) and redirects to external documentation, which may not be accessible offline or during website outages.
findings.evidence:
get_file_contents of CONTRIBUTING.md shows: '# Contributing to React

Want to contribute to React? There are a few things you need to know.

We wrote a **[contribution guide](https://reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html)** to help you get started.'
findings.recommendation: While external linking is common for large projects, consider adding key workflow steps (fork, branch, test, PR) directly in CONTRIBUTING.md as a quick reference, with the external link for comprehensive details.

findings.description: The project maintains an excellent release cadence with regular minor releases (3-6 months), major releases (12-18 months), and prompt patch releases for critical issues. CHANGELOG.md is exceptionally detailed with contributor attribution.
findings.evidence: CHANGELOG.md analysis shows: v19.2.1 (Dec 3, 2025), v19.2.0 (Oct 1, 2025), v19.1.2 (Dec 3, 2025), v19.1.1 (July 28, 2025), v19.1.0 (March 28, 2025), v19.0.0 (Dec 5, 2024). Each release includes breaking changes, new features, bug fixes, and contributor credits with GitHub handles and PR links. Security fixes documented (e.g., CVE-2018-6341 in multiple 16.x backports).
findings.recommendation: Continue exemplary release practices. Current cadence balances stability with feature delivery effectively.

findings.description: Recent issues receive initial triage labels within 24-48 hours, though some remain without comments for several days. Closed issues show active maintainer engagement rather than automated closure.
findings.evidence: Recent issue timestamps: #36327 created 2026-04-22T06:49:52Z with labels applied same day, #36320 created 2026-04-20T21:38:32Z updated 2026-04-20T21:40:06Z. Closed issue samples: #36329 created 2026-04-22, closed 2026-04-22 (1 day turnaround), #36302 created 2026-04-17, closed 2026-04-20 with 4 comments showing maintainer @mofeiZ engagement.
findings.recommendation: Current response time is good. Consider targeting <48 hour initial response for all new issues to maintain community confidence, particularly for first-time contributors.

findings.description: Complex issues receive substantial community discussion, with evidence of maintainer participation and thorough technical debate before resolution.
findings.evidence: Closed issue #24669 ('Bug: useId() not working inside <Suspense>') shows 47 comments over its lifetime. Issue #36315 (DevTools bug) has 2 comments with maintainer response within 2 days. Recent open issues show structured reporting with detailed reproduction steps and maintainer acknowledgment via labels.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current engagement levels. Consider GitHub Discussions to separate help requests from bug reports, reducing noise in issue tracker.

findings.description: A comprehensive Code of Conduct based on Contributor Covenant 1.4 is in place with clear enforcement contact and scope definitions.
findings.evidence: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md content shows: 'Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported by contacting the project team at <opensource-conduct@fb.com>.' Document includes standards for acceptable behavior, enforcement responsibilities, and scope covering both project spaces and community representation.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Code of Conduct follows industry best practices.

findings.description: A security policy file (SECURITY.md) is present, indicating established processes for vulnerability disclosure.
findings.evidence: get_repository_tree shows SECURITY.md (400 bytes) in root directory.
findings.recommendation: Verify that SECURITY.md includes clear vulnerability reporting instructions and expected response times. Consider periodic security audits given the project's widespread usage.

findings.description: The project maintains clear versioning and changelog practices, facilitating dependency management for downstream consumers.
findings.evidence: CHANGELOG.md shows systematic version progression: 0.x → 15.x → 16.x → 17.x → 18.x → 19.x with clear breaking change documentation. ReactVersions.js file present (1994 bytes). package.json present (8657 bytes) indicating npm package maintenance.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices. Ensure breaking changes are prominently documented in both CHANGELOG and release notes.

findings.description: Excellent README with comprehensive coverage of installation, usage, and contribution guidelines. The README is well-structured with badges, clear sections, and links to detailed documentation.
findings.evidence: README.md (5,317 bytes, SHA: 88f8e827b0255cf639043c83833c7a0c40ec0991) contains: installation guides (Quick Start, Add React to Existing Project, Create New React App), working code example (HelloMessage component with createRoot), contribution section with links to Code of Conduct and contributing guide, documentation structure with links to Tutorial, API Reference, etc. Includes npm version, license, and CI badges.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current high standards. README serves as an excellent template for other open-source projects.

findings.description: CONTRIBUTING.md file is minimal (only 208 bytes) and primarily serves as a redirect to external documentation. While the external guide is comprehensive, in-repository setup instructions would improve developer experience.
findings.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md (208 bytes, SHA: 589af800fdc36dd1658ea6e96ad88a60572ca523) contains only: 'Want to contribute to React? There are a few things you need to know. We wrote a contribution guide to help you get started.' with link to external documentation. CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (3,541 bytes) is present and comprehensive.
findings.recommendation: Expand CONTRIBUTING.md to include basic setup steps (clone, npm install, npm test, npm run build) inline, while maintaining the link to the comprehensive external guide for detailed information.

findings.description: Exceptional changelog quality with comprehensive version history from 2013 to present. Each release is well-documented with categorization, contributor attribution, and links to pull requests.
findings.evidence: CHANGELOG.md (265,863 bytes, SHA: 5953fafc278638db7932f59e28555c2d52a80a06) contains detailed entries for versions 19.2.1 (Dec 2025) through 0.3.0 (2013). Entries are categorized by package (React, React DOM, React Server Components), include breaking changes, new features, deprecations, bug fixes, with PR references (e.g., #35277) and contributor attribution (e.g., @sebmarkbage). Major releases link to blog posts and upgrade guides.
findings.recommendation: Continue current practices. This changelog serves as a gold standard for open-source projects.

findings.description: API documentation is primarily hosted externally at react.dev. While JSDoc comments exist in source files (54 instances of @param found), there is no dedicated docs/ directory within the repository for offline reference.
findings.evidence: Search for '@param' in JavaScript files returned 54 results including packages/react/src/ReactChildren.js, ReactBaseClasses.js, ReactNoopUpdateQueue.js. No docs/ directory found in repository tree (root tree contains 36 items including fixtures/, packages/, scripts/ but no docs/). README references external documentation at react.dev with sections: Quick Start, Tutorial, API Reference, Advanced Guides.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a docs/ directory with generated API documentation or markdown files for core APIs, even if react.dev remains the canonical source. This would improve offline discoverability and provide a backup reference within the repository.

findings.description: Basic usage examples present in README, and fixtures/ directory exists for testing. However, no dedicated examples/ directory with standalone runnable examples. Most examples are hosted externally at react.dev.
findings.evidence: Root tree shows fixtures/ directory (type: tree, SHA: d28cc220944841af5c8f569fd6d3ddbbdc65d47b). README.md contains basic HelloMessage component example. README states 'We have several examples on the website' with links to react.dev. No dedicated examples/ directory found in repository structure.
findings.recommendation: Add an examples/ directory with 3-5 standalone, runnable examples covering common use cases (basic component, hooks, state management, routing). This would complement the external tutorials and provide quick-start templates for developers.

findings.description: Source code uses JSDoc-style documentation and Flow type annotations extensively, providing strong type safety and inline documentation.
findings.evidence: Search results show 54 instances of @param JSDoc tags across JavaScript files. Flow type definitions present (scripts/flow/environment.js exists). Core React source files in packages/react/src/ include ReactChildren.js, ReactBaseClasses.js, ReactNoopUpdateQueue.js with documented functions.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining JSDoc comments and type annotations. Consider adding TypeScript definition files (.d.ts) if not already present for improved IDE support.

findings.description: Excellent project metadata with comprehensive files supporting the documentation ecosystem.
findings.evidence: Repository tree includes: LICENSE (1,088 bytes - MIT), SECURITY.md (400 bytes), CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (3,541 bytes), .editorconfig (229 bytes), package.json (8,657 bytes), README.md (5,317 bytes). All standard documentation files are present and properly sized, indicating substantial content.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current comprehensive metadata. All essential files are present and appropriately detailed.

findings.description: React employs a dual documentation strategy: minimal in-repository docs with comprehensive external documentation at react.dev. This is appropriate for a mature, large-scale library.
findings.evidence: README.md extensively links to react.dev for: Quick Start, Tutorial (Tic-Tac-Toe), Thinking in React, Installation guides, API Reference, Advanced Guides, Community support. CONTRIBUTING.md links to legacy.reactjs.org/docs/how-to-contribute.html. This pattern observed consistently across documentation files.
findings.recommendation: Current strategy is effective for production use. For improved developer experience, consider maintaining a docs/ directory with essential reference material for offline/airgapped development scenarios.

mcp_params.query: repo:facebook/react path:packages/react/src license OR copyright language:JavaScript

findings.description: The repository contains a clear, properly formatted MIT License file in the root directory. The license text is complete and standard-compliant, with copyright assigned to Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
findings.evidence:
LICENSE file (SHA: b93be90515ccd0b9daedaa589e42bf5929693f1f) contains full MIT License text: 'MIT License

Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software...'
findings.recommendation: No action required. The LICENSE file meets all OSI and SPDX standards for the MIT License.

findings.description: The root package.json file does not contain a 'license' field, which is standard practice in the npm/yarn ecosystem. While the file indicates 'private: true' (monorepo root), the absence of explicit license metadata reduces discoverability.
findings.evidence: package.json (SHA: 0ae925a1d4ecd7389a21d953ed05e151c9b03dbc) content shows: '{"private": true, "workspaces": ["packages/*"], "devDependencies": {...}}' - no 'license' field present.
findings.recommendation: Add '"license": "MIT"' to the root package.json file to align with npm ecosystem conventions and improve metadata completeness, even for private monorepo roots.

findings.description: Source files include consistent, well-formatted license headers that reference the MIT License and copyright holder. Headers include copyright notice, license type, and pointer to the full LICENSE file.
findings.evidence:
packages/react/index.js (SHA: 78b11b809e7520e342a1fa05d10d264629d610c3) header: '/**
 * Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
 *
 * This source code is licensed under the MIT license found in the
 * LICENSE file in the root directory of this source tree.
 *
 * @flow
 */'
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining consistent license headers across all source files to ensure MIT License compliance requirements are met.

findings.description: All declared devDependencies use permissive licenses (MIT, Apache-2.0, ISC) that are fully compatible with the project's MIT License. No copyleft (GPL/LGPL) dependencies detected.
findings.evidence: package.json devDependencies include: @babel/* (MIT), eslint (MIT), jest (MIT), typescript (Apache-2.0), rollup (MIT), prettier (MIT), flow-bin (MIT) - all permissive licenses compatible with MIT.
findings.recommendation: Continue monitoring dependency licenses during updates. Consider implementing automated license checking tools (e.g., license-checker, FOSSA) for transitive dependency analysis.

findings.description: No NOTICE, THIRD-PARTY-LICENSES, or ATTRIBUTIONS file was found in the repository root. While not required for MIT License compliance, such files are best practice for large open-source projects with external contributions or vendored code.
findings.evidence: Repository tree (SHA: 142cfde89edab3d4eabd6335458b4c8736cebfb6) contains: LICENSE, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG.md, README.md, MAINTAINERS - but no NOTICE or THIRD-PARTY file detected.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a NOTICE or THIRD-PARTY-LICENSES.md file to document any vendored third-party code, external contributions, or acknowledgments, especially if planning to incorporate Apache-2.0 licensed components.

findings.description: Copyright notices are consistent across all examined files, properly attributing copyright to 'Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates', which provides clear legal ownership and rights assignment.
findings.evidence: LICENSE file states 'Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.' and packages/react/index.js header states 'Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.' - consistent copyright holder across files.
findings.recommendation: Maintain this consistency in all future source files and ensure contributors understand the copyright assignment through CONTRIBUTING.md.

findings.description: The repository includes comprehensive governance documentation (CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, MAINTAINERS) which enhances legal clarity and establishes clear processes for community participation.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows presence of: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, MAINTAINERS files at root level - all supporting legal and community governance.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining these governance files and keep them updated as project policies evolve.

findings.description: The MIT License is one of the most permissive open-source licenses, allowing commercial use, modification, distribution, and private use with minimal restrictions. This choice maximizes adoption potential and minimizes legal friction.
findings.evidence: LICENSE file contains standard MIT License text with all required clauses: permission grant, conditions (copyright notice requirement), warranty disclaimer, and liability limitation.
findings.recommendation: The MIT License is appropriate for this project's goals. No change recommended unless business requirements shift toward requiring patent grants (Apache-2.0) or copyleft enforcement (GPL).
